description: Epic 1 — Complete development infrastructure: SQLite schema, repository CRUD, configuration system, CI/CD pipeline, and test fixtures (Stories 1.3–1.7)

userStories.title: SQLite database setup and V1 migration
userStories.description: As a Seshat developer, I want the SQLite database struct with WAL mode and refinery migrations so that the persistence layer is ready for use.
acceptanceCriteria: seshat-storage Cargo.toml depends on rusqlite (bundled, modern_sqlite features) and refinery (rusqlite feature)
Database struct wraps Arc<Mutex<Connection>> for writes
Database::open(path) creates/opens SQLite DB with PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL
Database::open(path) auto-applies migrations via embed_migrations!
V1__initial_schema.sql creates nodes table (id, branch_id, nature, weight, confidence, adoption_count, total_count, description, ext_data JSON)
V1__initial_schema.sql creates edges table (id, source_id REFERENCES nodes, target_id REFERENCES nodes, edge_type, branch_id, weight, metadata JSON)
V1__initial_schema.sql creates files_ir table (id, branch_id, file_path, language, content_hash, ir_data BLOB, updated_at, UNIQUE(branch_id, file_path))
V1__initial_schema.sql creates metadata table (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL)
Indexes created: idx_nodes_branch_id, idx_nodes_nature, idx_edges_source_id, idx_edges_target_id, idx_files_ir_branch_path
Unit test: migration applies cleanly on fresh in-memory DB
Unit test: re-opening existing DB is idempotent
cargo test -p seshat-storage passes
cargo build passes for entire workspace
userStories.notes: ADR-5 (refinery migrations), ADR-19 (connection management). Migration files in crates/seshat-storage/migrations/. Use embed_migrations! macro.

userStories.title: NodeRepository and EdgeRepository traits and SQLite implementations
userStories.description: As a Seshat developer, I want repository traits and SQLite implementations for nodes and edges so that knowledge graph data can be persisted and queried.
acceptanceCriteria: NodeRepository trait with methods: insert, get_by_id, find_by_nature, find_by_branch, update, delete
EdgeRepository trait with methods: insert, find_by_source, find_by_target, find_by_type, delete
SQLite implementations for both traits in repository/ submodule
All writes use parameterized queries within transactions
Unit tests cover insert, read back, update, delete, and not-found for nodes
Unit tests cover insert, query by source/target/type, and delete for edges
cargo test -p seshat-storage passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (Database struct and schema). Types from seshat-core: KnowledgeNode, Edge, NodeId, EdgeId, BranchId, KnowledgeNature, EdgeType.

userStories.title: FileIRRepository and BranchRepository traits and SQLite implementations
userStories.description: As a Seshat developer, I want repository traits and SQLite implementations for files_ir and branches so that parsed IR and branch snapshots can be managed.
acceptanceCriteria: FileIRRepository trait with methods: upsert, get_by_path, get_by_branch, delete_by_path, check_content_hash
BranchRepository trait with methods: create_snapshot, switch_branch, delete_branch, list_branches, get_current_branch
SQLite implementations for both traits in repository/ submodule
create_snapshot copies all nodes + edges + files_ir rows with new branch_id in a single transaction
Multi-row operations use transactions
Unit tests cover upsert (insert and update), get, delete, hash check for files_ir
Unit tests cover snapshot creation, branch switching, listing, and deletion
cargo test -p seshat-storage passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (Database + schema) and US-002 (node/edge repos for snapshot verification). BranchRepository stores current branch in metadata table.

userStories.title: Configuration system with seshat.toml and zero-config defaults
userStories.description: As a Seshat developer, I want a configuration loading system that reads seshat.toml with sensible defaults so that Seshat works zero-config out of the box.
acceptanceCriteria: seshat-bin Cargo.toml depends on toml crate and dirs crate (for XDG paths)
AppConfig struct in seshat-bin/src/config.rs with sections: scan, detection, server, watcher, backup, cache, embedding (Option)
All config sub-structs implement Default and use #[serde(default)]
AppConfig::load() searches for seshat.toml in current directory then $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/seshat/
No config file found: defaults used (zero-config works)
Partial config file: present values override defaults, missing keys use defaults
Environment variable SESHAT_LOG overrides server.log_level
seshat.toml.example created in repo root with all options commented out and defaults documented
Unit tests: default config, config from TOML string, partial config merge, env var override
cargo test -p seshat-bin passes
cargo build passes for entire workspace
userStories.notes: WatcherConfig, BackupConfig, CacheConfig are new config structs not yet in seshat-core. Define them in seshat-core/src/config.rs or locally in seshat-bin.

userStories.title: CI/CD pipeline with fmt, clippy, test, and conventional commits
userStories.description: As a Seshat developer, I want CI/CD workflows so that code quality is enforced on every PR.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs on PRs: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test, cargo doc --no-deps, conventional commit validation
.github/workflows/lint-workflows.yml runs actionlint only on changes to .github/workflows/
All workflow YAML files are syntactically valid
cargo build passes for entire workspace
userStories.notes: CI only — no release workflow yet. Keep ci.yml simple with matrix for stable Rust on ubuntu-latest.

userStories.title: Release pipeline and version string with git hash
userStories.description: As a Seshat developer, I want automated releases and version output with git hash so that releases are consistent and traceable.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/release.yml uses release-plz for version bump, CHANGELOG.md generation, git tag, GitHub Release
Release includes cross-compiled binaries for x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-apple-darwin, aarch64-apple-darwin
build.rs in seshat-bin captures git commit hash via git rev-parse --short HEAD with fallback to unknown
seshat --version prints: seshat {version} ({hash})
cargo build passes for entire workspace
userStories.notes: build.rs uses std::process::Command. Set GIT_HASH via cargo:rustc-env. main.rs reads via env!() macro.

userStories.title: Pre-commit hooks configuration
userStories.description: As a Seshat developer, I want pre-commit hooks so that formatting and lint issues are caught before commit.
acceptanceCriteria: .pre-commit-config.yaml configures hooks: trailing-whitespace, end-of-file-fixer, check-yaml, check-toml, check-merge-conflict
.pre-commit-config.yaml includes conventional-pre-commit on commit-msg stage
.pre-commit-config.yaml includes local hooks for cargo fmt and cargo clippy
YAML file is syntactically valid
userStories.notes: No enforcement that devs install hooks — just the config file.

userStories.title: Rust fixture project with known conventions
userStories.description: As a Seshat developer, I want a reference Rust project so that integration tests can verify scanning and detection.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/fixtures/rust_project/ contains 10-15 .rs files with known patterns
Patterns include: thiserror error types, tracing logging, grouped imports, #[test] functions, pub/private visibility, derive macros
tests/fixtures/rust_project/Cargo.toml is a valid manifest with thiserror and tracing dependencies
tests/fixtures/rust_project/expected_conventions.json documents expected detector findings
All .rs files are syntactically valid Rust
crates/seshat-detectors/tests/fixtures/rust_samples/ contains at least 3 individual .rs sample files
userStories.notes: Files must be syntactically valid for Tree-sitter but don't need to compile (no full dependency resolution).

userStories.title: TypeScript fixture project with known conventions
userStories.description: As a Seshat developer, I want a reference TypeScript project so that integration tests can verify TS scanning and detection.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/fixtures/typescript_project/ contains 10-15 .ts/.tsx files with known patterns
Patterns include: barrel exports (index.ts), ESM imports, Jest tests, custom error classes, type-only imports
tests/fixtures/typescript_project/package.json is a valid manifest
tests/fixtures/typescript_project/expected_conventions.json documents expected detector findings
All .ts files are syntactically valid TypeScript
crates/seshat-detectors/tests/fixtures/typescript_samples/ contains at least 3 individual .ts sample files
userStories.notes: Files must be syntactically valid for Tree-sitter but don't need to compile (no tsc/node).

userStories.title: Python fixture project with known conventions
userStories.description: As a Seshat developer, I want a reference Python project so that integration tests can verify Python scanning and detection.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/fixtures/python_project/ contains 10-15 .py files with known patterns
Patterns include: stdlib logging, grouped imports, pytest tests, type hints, __all__ exports, __init__.py files
tests/fixtures/python_project/pyproject.toml is a valid manifest
tests/fixtures/python_project/expected_conventions.json documents expected detector findings
All .py files are syntactically valid Python
crates/seshat-detectors/tests/fixtures/python_samples/ contains at least 3 individual .py sample files
userStories.notes: Files must be syntactically valid for Tree-sitter but don't need to run (no Python interpreter).
